title: quercus cambodiensis
intro: Quercus cambodiensis, also known as the Cambodian oak or Cambodian evergreen oak, is a species of evergreen tree in the family Fagaceae, native to Cambodia and parts of Southeast Asia. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Quercus cambodiensis include its potential to reduce oxidative stress, improve cardiovascular health, and support immune function, while also exhibiting antimicrobial and antifungal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is often used to prepare infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, and is also used in the production of herbal supplements and topical creams.
